title: Cirrus: Support testing with F31
body:
Add F31 + Drop F29

***Depends on: #3754 #4028 #4028 #4154 #4222 #4825***

Fixes #5285
Fixes #3447

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body:
LGTM.

The F31 packages will have to be tested with crun, not runc.  Or we need to disable cgroupsV2.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3728) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

> The F31 packages will have to be tested with crun, not runc. Or we need to disable cgroupsV2.

Thanks, I half-guessed at this.  I'm thinking we can drop the cgroupsv2/crun task, since having F31 in the mix will satisfy these neeeds.  @giuseppe is that right, or is there reason to continue testing either on F30?
ewww, errr, crap `sudo: dnf: command not found` that's bad.  What on earth have I broken...

F31 VM setup packaging failures:


***All: Earliest working F31 cache-image build is `fedora-31-libpod-5654728358232064` that should fire up successfully with `hack/get_ci_vm.sh`.  Please play!***

(yes `setup_fedora.sh` is currently a big mess)
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3817) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3581) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Note to me:  We need both runc and crun testing happening in F31 and F30.
runc tests will not pass in F31 until runc supports cgroupsv2 or you need to boot the kernel in cgroupv1 mode
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4042) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

> or you need to boot the kernel in cgroupv1 mode

Yeah, that's what I'm worried about needing to do.  The only way that's possible (the booting part) is by using a custom, snowflake image - a.k.a. a PITA.

We currently do this for giuseppe's playground (`$SPECIAL_FEDORA_CACHE_IMAGE_NAME`) in F30 and I was hoping to be able to drop that in this PR...now it sounds like I need to ADD another snowflake image (the 'P' part of the PITA).  It complicates automation and ongoing maintenance - and therefor impacts reliability, and flakes, and sleepless nights, and ... :disappointed: 

Maybe we can "strategically" decide to not ship **runc** with F31 and drop **crun** from F30 :smile_cat:

@rhatdan is there significant risk added if we were to only test crun in F31 and only test runc in F30?
(with the understanding we may need to swap testing to runc in F31 later if patches are accepted)

note to me: Still need to address the what/how/where of `crun` and `runc`.  Last commit does:

* Test crun in F31
* Test runc in F30 and Ubuntu
/hold
I am fine with only testing crun on f31 and runc on f30.  Once we get to f31 and f32 and runc has support for cgroupfs, we can start testing with both.
Got a new F31 cloud-image on the download site, produced a new F31 base image.  Rolling new cache-images now.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4087) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4090) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
*note to me*: Need new crun 0.10 to prevent VM lockups and test timeouts
note to me: Ubuntu failures expected (no builds happening w/ packaged conmon).  Need #4222 to help investigate system-test failures (varlink process died?)
Ref: [image build task w/ hanging F31 VM](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3901/checks?check_run_id=260963375)
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3792) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

***grumble grumble grumble*** Even with the official F31 cloud images, I've got VMs locking up during integration testing (different test every time).  For example: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6425946084605952

Oddly, it always seems to happen about 20-25minutes into testing :confounded:
linked https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1767539 kernel panic in F31
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4384) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Attempting to avoid kernel panic by switching to deadline scheduler on F31.
/hold cancel

@giuseppe @haircommander mind taking a peek at these two F31 rootless failures (they're similar)?

* [special_testing_rootless TEST_REMOTE_CLIENT:false](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5471569836507136/logs/integration_test.log)
* [special_testing_rootless TEST_REMOTE_CLIENT:true](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/6034519789928448/logs/integration_test.log)

Note: I already temporarily disabled [podman-in-podman testing because of massive numbers of failures](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6024371776258048).  So debugging that will need to come also / after this PR merges.  I'm also mentioning this in case it's related to the rootless test failures.
(Peter I mentioned you because I seen the word "conmon" in some of the error messages :smile: )
This pull request had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the pull request will be automatically closed within 7 days.
This is still something we want.
Status: Working on verifying the BFQ kernel patch (this PR reproduces the panic).  Will resume here once the fix is confirmed (and I no longer need this PR to repro the problem)...

...no dice, patch does not fix problem.  Now reported/tracking upstream: https://bugzilla.kernel.org/show_bug.cgi?id=205447

To allow libpod to move forward, I'll put my workaround back in place, build fresh VM images, and go from there.  Then the kernel panic debugging can continue in parallel.
note to me:  PR took too long, it's F30 and F31 base images got pruned, need to re-add them.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4836) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
note to self: need chroups systemd option, and rootless should install certs before becoming a user.

@edsantiago tried to catch ya on IRC.  I'm whittling down through the last few problems holding us back from F31 support in libpod PR CI.  Current focus is on the 'special_testing_rootless TEST_REMOTE_CLIENT:true' that is reporting a "timeout" failure coming from the system-tests.
I've re-run this part of the matrix a few times, and the failure seems repeatable (and so I'm doubting a service timeout is the root-cause).

Prior to hitting this reported "timeout" in the system-tests, the same job (F31 + rootless + remote) was failing much earlier during the integration tests.  Matt suggested setting `export CGROUP_MANAGER=systemd` and this solved the problem (the ginkgo tests are sensitive to this env. var).  I do **not** believe that env. var. value is preserved for the subsequent step of system-tests (but I can make it so, if need be).

Any idea if this "timeout" indicates another problem and/or could be related to not using "--cgroup-manager systemd" on the podman command line for the system-tests?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4923) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
(Rebased and enabled two more F31 tests).
(Rebased and updated setup to force `OCI_RUNTIME=/usr/bin/crun`)

@edsantiago sorry, the reference to the error got lost, here's it again:  https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5793941895249920

```
...all ok so far...
[+0124s] not ok 43 podman kill - test signal handling in containers
[+0124s] # (from function `die' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 274,
[+0124s] #  from function `kill_and_check' in file test/system/130-kill.bats, line 40,
[+0124s] #  in test file test/system/130-kill.bats, line 46)
[+0124s] #   `kill_and_check $s' failed
...sig 2 is failing...
[+0124s] # $ ./bin/podman-remote kill -s 2 ddb22f856b7a04f0d01018bc2fb0ec78b9b98453fdfc1cc6a5079759d09bd8a7
[+0124s] # ddb22f856b7a04f0d01018bc2fb0ec78b9b98453fdfc1cc6a5079759d09bd8a7
[+0124s] # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
[+0124s] # #| FAIL: Timed out: no ACK for kill -s 2
[+0124s] # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

I poked at this and am not sure what to make of it, it only seems to happen w/ F31 + podman-remote system-testing.  I've added a temporary commit to remove signal '2' in this case, as an experiment to see if the subsequent `kill_and_check -INT      2` behaves differently.  I also wonder if maybe, `podman-remote` is gobbling up the signal and not passing it on to the container?
Found bug #5004
note to me: Check last run for affect of enabling selinux on F31
@edsantiago un-ping, I think we have this signal problem figured out.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #5044) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
/approve

LGTM
/lgtm
